title: feat: route more Gemini models to Airforce with Vertex fallback
author: voodoohop
pr_body_excerpt:
Routes three more Gemini models primarily through Airforce (cheaper resale of the same SKU), falling back to Vertex on failure — extending the already-live `gemini` (gemini-3-flash) route.

- `gemini-large` (3.1-pro), `gemini-flash-lite-3.1`, `gemini-3.5-flash` → Airforce primary / Vertex fallback
- Same registry modelId on both targets → billing unchanged; Airforce-served calls cost us 20–50% less
- Injects an empty system prompt (only when the user sends none) on every Airforce-routed Gemini model, to displace the coding-agent persona the resold backend occasionally defaults to. Verified: ~0 added tokens (prompt_tokens 14→14/16) and 4/4 clean identity reps; user-supplied system prompts left untouched.
- Relaxes `createSystemPromptTransform` to accept `""` (a deliberate empty value).

Why not Claude (this PR originally proposed it): parity testing showed Airforce resells Claude through coding-agent harnesses (Kiro / Claude Code) that an empty — and even a full — system prompt does not reliably suppress (sonnet/opus leaked 4/4 with `""`); opus is also billed for Airforce's own ~1,400-token injected prompt. Gemini's persona leak is rare and fully fixed by the empty-prompt transform, so only Gemini is expanded.

Tested live against `portkey.myceli.ai` across modes (stream, tools, image) + identity reps. `tsc` clean, biome formatted, 15 tests pass.

gist.headline: Gemini gets a new flight path
gist.blurb: We've optimized the routing for our Gemini models, sending them through a more efficient backend while keeping Vertex as a reliable fallback. Plus, we fixed a quirky persona leak along the way!
gist.summary: Routes gemini-large, gemini-flash-lite-3.1, and gemini-3.5-flash through Airforce as the primary provider with Vertex as a fallback, reducing internal costs while maintaining reliability.
gist.impact: Users will experience the same Gemini models with improved backend efficiency, and an injected empty system prompt ensures the models no longer accidentally adopt a coding-agent persona.
gist.visual_concept: Polly the bee as an air traffic controller, directing glowing data packets (representing Gemini models) along a green 'Airforce' flight path, with a backup 'Vertex' runway visible in the background.
gist.image_prompt: Cozy pixel art scene of Polly the bee wearing a tiny air traffic controller headset, holding glowing orange batons to guide glowing data packets along a lime green (#ecf874) runway. A secondary pastel runway sits in the background. Soft pastel gradient sky, chunky retro sprites, warm lighting, lo-fi vibes like Stardew Valley. Nostalgic but beautiful, emotionally warm.
